items.title: Xiaomi 开源 MiMo-V2.5-Pro (1.02T MoE, 强 Agent 编码)
items.summary: 4 月 28 日小米开源 MiMo-V2.5 系列(MIT)。Pro 版 1.02T/42B 激活,混合注意力(128-token 滑窗 : 全局 = 6:1,KV cache ~7×↓),1M 上下文。配 MiMo Code agent harness,厂商基准在 SWE-bench Verified(82 vs 79)/Pro(62 vs 55)/Terminal-Bench 2(73 vs 69)超 Claude Code+Sonnet 4.6。尚无 Ascend 移植。数字均厂商口径,provisional。

items.title: Seed 2.1 Pro 抢先版上线 LMArena (闭源, 编码/Agent)
items.summary: 6 月 19 日 ByteDance 把 Seed-2.1-Pro-Preview 放上 Code Arena 试用:前端编码子榜 1539 Elo、全球第 8,约等于 Claude Opus 4.6。闭源 API、无 Ascend 移植——是标杆而非 NPU 载荷。完整基准随正式版未来几周公布。分析见 Blog → Dispatch 01。

items.title: 新一批开源模型 (6月中): Qwen3 Coder Next · MiniMax M2.7 · Nemotron 3 Ultra
items.summary: 6 月中又一波开放权重发布:Qwen3 Coder Next、MiniMax M2.5/M2.7 Highspeed、NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra 等(新闻聚合,基准/细节待官方)。

items.title: DeepSeek-V4 & GLM-5 run on Ascend 910B (vLLM-Ascend)
items.summary: vLLM-Ascend's 2026 support matrix adds DeepSeek-V4 (custom 910B ops, MTP KV-cache sharding for spec decoding) and GLM-5 (W8A8C8 quant); Qwen3.5 + W8A8 across Qwen3/DeepSeek/MiniMax. MiniMax-M3 not yet listed.

items.title: GLM-5.2 open-sourced (MIT, 1M context)
items.summary: Launched Jun 13 2026; MIT open weights now live on Hugging Face (zai-org/GLM-5.2). 753B params (GLM-5.1-class 744B MoE / 40B active), context 200K→1M, max output 131K, coding-first. No official benchmarks at launch; media cites it edging GPT-5.5 on long-horizon coding (FrontierSWE/SWE-Marathon) at ~1/6 cost — treat as provisional.
items.ascendNote: GLM-5 is supported on vLLM-Ascend (W8A8C8 quant); GLM-5.2 not yet listed by name.

items.title: MiniMax-M3 with MSA sparse attention
items.summary: Open-weight 1M-context model, native multimodal + agentic coding. MSA (blockwise sparse on GQA) cuts attention compute ~28.4x at 1M context. Official report on arXiv.
items.ascendNote: MiniMax family has W8A8/QuaRot on vLLM-Ascend (M2.x); M3 not yet listed by name.

items.title: DeepSeek-V4 (1.6T MoE, 1M context)
items.summary: V4-Pro/Flash with hybrid sparse attention (CSA+HCA), Multi-Token Prediction, and a multi-teacher distillation pipeline over 32T+ tokens. Official tech report PDF on Hugging Face.
items.ascendNote: Supported on Ascend 910B via vLLM-Ascend (custom ops, MTP KV-cache sharding for speculative decoding).

items.title: Agentic RL: a distinct paradigm (survey)
items.summary: Survey reframing agentic RL vs single-turn RLVR — multi-turn credit assignment, tool/environment interaction, long-horizon planning.

items.title: Stable Asynchrony: variance-controlled off-policy RL
items.summary: Controls heavy-tailed importance weights from stale rollouts to keep async/off-policy GRPO stable at high asynchrony.

items.title: Ascend 950PR / 950DT announced (FP8, self-built HBM)
items.summary: Next-gen Ascend with native FP8/MXFP4 and Huawei's own HBM — 950PR (inference) ~Q1 2026, 950DT (training) ~Q4 2026. Opens the door to FP8 RL on Ascend.

items.title: vLLM-Ascend v0.13.0 released
items.summary: Latest Ascend rollout backend — broader model coverage and performance; the rollout engine RL stacks target on NPU.

items.title: DeepSeek-V3.2 + Sparse Attention (DSA)
items.summary: Open-weight frontier model with trainable sparse attention for cheaper long-context; RL post-training continues the R1 lineage.

items.title: GDRO-RL: distributionally robust GRPO
items.summary: Rollout-/Prompt-GDRO reweight toward worst-case prompt distributions on top of GRPO for more robust reasoning RL.

items.title: MindSpeed-RL: Distributed RL Post-Training on Ascend NPU
items.summary: Native distributed GRPO-family RL on Ascend, with experiments up to DeepSeek-R1-MoE-671B on a 384-NPU superpod — the strongest large-scale RL-on-Ascend evidence to date.

items.title: AsyncFlow: Asynchronous Streaming RL Post-Training
items.summary: An async streaming RL framework targeting the throughput limits of synchronous/colocated RL — directly relevant to NPU constraints like the missing vLLM sleep mode.

items.title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Pure large-scale RL (GRPO + verifiable rewards) elicits emergent reasoning with no SFT (R1-Zero); catalyzed the 2025 RLVR wave.

items.title: GSPO: Group Sequence Policy Optimization
items.summary: Sequence-level importance ratios and clipping fix GRPO instability and notably stabilize MoE RL; contributed to Qwen3.

items.title: DAPO: An Open-Source LLM RL System at Scale
items.summary: GRPO + Clip-Higher, dynamic sampling, token-level loss, and overlong reward shaping; 50 on AIME 2024 with Qwen2.5-32B at half the steps.

items.title: ProRL: Prolonged RL Expands Reasoning Boundaries
items.summary: Stabilizes very long RL runs (KL control, reference resetting) and shows prolonged RL uncovers genuinely new reasoning strategies.

items.title: MiniMax-M1: Test-Time Compute with Lightning Attention (CISPO)
items.summary: Open hybrid-attention reasoner trained with CISPO (clips IS-weights, not tokens); full RL on 512 H800s in ~3 weeks.

items.title: AReaL: Large-Scale Asynchronous RL for Language Reasoning
items.summary: Fully decouples rollout from training with staleness-enhanced PPO for ~2.77x speedup; now ships a stable Ascend branch.

items.title: Native Sparse Attention (NSA)
items.summary: Trainable, hardware-aligned hierarchical sparse attention matching full attention with large speedups at 64K length.

items.title: The Entropy Mechanism of RL for Reasoning LLMs
items.summary: Explains policy-entropy collapse via log-prob/advantage covariance and proposes Clip-Cov / KL-Cov to sustain exploration.

items.title: Pretraining LLMs with NVFP4
items.summary: Large-scale 4-bit (NVFP4) pretraining of a 12B model on 10T tokens matching FP8 — the most ambitious FP4 training run reported.

items.title: GLM-4.5: Agentic, Reasoning, and Coding Foundation Models
items.summary: Hybrid thinking/direct MoE (355B/32B) trained with a difficulty-based RL curriculum on the open-source 'slime' framework.

items.title: Actor as Its Own Critic: Unifying Region Understanding and Localization via CycleGRPO
items.summary: This paper introduces Actor as Its Own Critic, a unified reinforcement learning framework, Cycle Group Relative Policy Optimization (CycleGRPO), that jointly optimizes region understanding and localization for Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). Unlike existing separate pipelines, we leverage …

items.title: SCOPE-RL: Optimizing Reasoning Paths Before and After Success
items.summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) optimizes LLMs using sparse verifiable final-answer rewards. This sparse anchor reliably verifies whether a trajectory succeeds but provides no direct feedback on the reasoning path that produced it. Before success, prerequisite progress on hard …

items.title: EasyOPD: An Easy-to-use On-Policy Distillation Framework for Large Language Models
items.summary: Conventional language-model distillation often relies on fixed teacher-generated data, which may not cover the states encountered by an evolving student policy. On-policy distillation (OPD) instead collects teacher or evaluator supervision on student-generated rollouts. However, existing OPD methods…

items.title: SCALECUA: Scaling Computer Use Agents with Verifiable Task Synthesis and Efficient Online RL
items.summary: Computer use agents (CUAs) are emerging as a powerful interface for automating complex digital workflows through visual perception and GUI execution. Online reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has emerged as a key direction for scaling their capabilities. However, this paradigm is …

items.title: When the Reward Suite Is Leaky: A Preregistered Causal Contrast of Natural Verifier False Positives in RLVR
items.summary: The test suites used as RLVR rewards for code have natural false positives: per-task, persistent, asymmetric errors that accept the same wrong programs every time they appear, unlike the symmetric or resampled noise assumed by existing noise-robustness analyses. We run a preregistered two-arm causal…

items.title: Extending LLM Context via Associative Recurrent Memory
items.summary: Extending the context length of large language models (LLMs) is critical for many real-world applications, yet standard transformers remain constrained by quadratic compute and linear memory scaling. In this work, we investigate the Associative Recurrent Memory Transformer (ARMT) as a practical appr…

items.title: [AAFLOW+] Stateful Operator Abstraction with Zero-Copy Distributed KV Cache Orchestration for Multi-Agent Workflows
items.summary: Multi-agent LLM systems increasingly integrate retrieval, planning, and reasoning, but remain fundamentally text-centric, requiring agents to repeatedly recompute shared context through expensive prefill. Although single-request inference is known to be accelerated by KV-cache management, it is usua…

items.title: HCRMap: Pressure-Aware Hot-Expert Residency Mapping for 3.5D MoE Chiplet Inference
items.summary: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) large language models (LLM) activate only a small number of experts during inference, but token routing introduces persistent expert hotness skew: a small set of hot experts continuously receives most tokens, while the remaining experts are lightly loaded. On 3.5D multi-chip…

items.title: UMoE:Unlocking Every Expert in Domain-Specific Training
items.summary: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models scale capacity without proportional compute cost and have become a key architecture for frontier large language models (LLMs). Yet domain-specific post-training inherits an expert pool shaped by mixed-domain pre-training: a substantial subset of experts contributes li…

items.title: SETA: Scaling Environments for Terminal Agents
items.summary: Large language models (LLMs) are rapidly shifting toward agents that solve tasks through diverse interfaces, including web and graphical user interfaces (GUIs). Among these, the terminal command line provides a text-based, general-purpose interface, covering tasks from system operations to data scie…

items.title: Predictive Divergence Masks for LLM RL
items.summary: Reinforcement learning for large language models (LLMs) typically relies on trust-region masks to stabilize off-policy updates. The dominant PPO-style approach uses the sampled-token importance ratio for two criteria: a proximity criterion, which asks whether the policy has moved too far from the be…

items.title: Filtering Harmful Actions Isn't Enough: Phantom Transfer in Agentic SDF
items.summary: Synthetic data is widely used to train large language models because it is inexpensive to generate and easy to control. As models are increasingly deployed as agents, synthetic trajectories are likely to become an important source of training data for agentic behavior. We investigate the effects of …

items.title: To Answer or to Abstain: Mitigating Search-Agent Hallucinations via Abstention-Aware Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Recent advances in equipping Large Language Models (LLMs) with search tools and outcome-reward reinforcement learning (RL) have achieved new state-of-the-art results on open-domain QA tasks. However, we argue that current training paradigms harbor a critical vulnerability: they predominantly reward …

items.title: Agentic-DPO: From Imitation to Agentic Policy Optimization on Expert Trajectories
items.summary: Large Language Model (LLM) agents are commonly trained from expert trajectories using supervised fine-tuning (SFT), which treats multi-turn agent behavior as ordinary text imitation. This recipe is simple and low-cost, but it only learns to imitate the sequence of expert actions, rather than trainin…

items.title: Tool-Adaptive LLM Reranker
items.summary: Generative Large Language Models (LLMs) have revolutionized information retrieval, yet their strictly parametric nature frequently leads to severe factual hallucinations when confronted with complex queries beyond their epistemic boundaries. While external tool-calling can mitigate this, indiscrimin…

items.title: MemDecay: Region-Aware KV Cache Eviction for Efficient LLM Agent Inference
items.summary: Large language model (LLM) agents accumulate heterogeneous context, including system instructions, plans, user turns, retrieved documents, tool outputs, and intermediate reasoning, whose key-value (KV) cache can become a major memory bottleneck. Existing eviction policies generally apply the same at…

items.title: Reliability Scaling Laws for Quantized Large Language Models
items.summary: Quantization is a powerful strategy to build capable and resource-efficient large language models (LLMs) by reducing the bitwidth of the parameters. While quantized LLMs achieve state-of-the-art performance on unperturbed inputs using standard predictive metrics, their performance on perturbed input…

items.title: ARMOR: Stabilizing On-Policy LLM RL with Off-Policy Anchor Samples
items.summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) has significantly enhanced the reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs), yet the training process remains notoriously fragile. In this work, we investigate a critical source of this instability: over-optimization, where models exploit training heuristics at …

items.title: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Physics: Post-training LLMs with Continuous Rewards
items.summary: Partial differential equations (PDEs) are foundational to modeling in science and engineering, but constructing reliable numerical solvers remains labor-intensive, demanding expert knowledge of discretization schemes, stability conditions, and boundary treatments. Recent work has begun to frame PDE …

items.title: GRASP: GRanularity-Aware Search Policy for Agentic RAG
items.summary: Agentic retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) extends static RAG by allowing language models to iteratively reason, generate search queries, retrieve evidence, and predict answers. However, it remains challenging for models to decide when to retrieve, whether to use lexical matching or semantic simil…

items.title: SynthDocBench: Controlled Benchmark for Long-Context Visual Document Understanding
items.summary: Vision language models (VLMs) have achieved strong performance on visual document understanding benchmarks such as DocVQA, ChartQA, and MMLongBench-Doc. However, real-world documents combine multiple factors such as length, layout complexity, modality, and question difficulty, which makes it difficu…

items.title: FlashAccel: Leveraging High-Bandwidth Flash for High-Throughput LLM Inference
items.summary: Large language model (LLM) inference is increasingly limited by the capacity of High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM) in GPUs, as model weights and KV cache grow rapidly. High-Bandwidth Flash (HBF) provides higher capacity than HBM while retaining comparable bandwidth, making it a promising substrate for capa…

items.title: Workload-Driven Optimization for On-Device Real-Time Subtitle Translation
items.summary: This report studies on-device English-to-Traditional-Chinese subtitle translation for Taiwan under short inputs, short outputs, batch-size-one inference, low latency, and privacy constraints. These conditions limit the value of optimizations designed for long-context or high-throughput language-mode…

items.title: Silent Failures in Quantized LLM Reasoning: A Taxonomy-Based Analysis of Hollow Convergence and Failure Mode Shifts
items.summary: We show that post-training quantization can silently alter how large language models reason even when task accuracy is preserved. Using a six-category failure taxonomy validated by two independent human annotators (Cohen's $κ$ = 0.906), we classify 30,000 chain-of-thought outputs from five instructi…

items.title: A Sovereign, Open-Source Foundation Model for German and English
items.summary: We present Soofi S 30B-A3B, a sovereign, open-source Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) hybrid Mamba Transformer foundation model for German and English. Its hybrid design activates only 3B of 30B parameters per token and keeps the inference cache near-constant as context grows, giving it a decisive throughpu…

items.title: On the Limitations of Non-GPU AI Accelerators for Large-Model Inference: A Field Study of MoE and Multimodal Serving on Huawei Ascend
items.summary: Non-GPU AI accelerators are increasingly adopted as alternatives to general-purpose GPUs for large-model inference, but the real engineering cost of migrating demanding workloads beyond CUDA remains poorly documented. We present a field study of deploying two large inference workloads on a 16-device…

items.title: BiSCo-LLM: Lookup-Free Binary Spherical Coding for Extreme Low-Bit Large Language Model Compression
items.summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly constrained by memory capacity, weight bandwidth, and checkpoint storage during deployment. Existing low-bit compression methods mainly follow two directions. Scalar or group-wise quantization is simple and compatible with efficient low-precision kernels…

items.title: GIFT: Geometry-Informed Low-precision Gradient Communication for LLM Pretraining
items.summary: Gradient communication is a primary scaling bottleneck in large language model (LLM) pretraining. Communicating gradients in low-precision formats, such as FP8 and NVFP4, can significantly reduce the communication volume. Existing methods quantize gradients via linear or nonlinear mappings in Euclid…

items.title: Full-Stack FP4: Stable LLM Pretraining with Quantized Projections, Optimizers, and Attention
items.summary: Recent NVFP4 pretraining methods mainly target transformer linear layers, leaving optimizer states, optimizer arithmetic and attention underexplored in 4-bit pipelines. This critical gap blocks stable full-stack 4-bit pretraining, as the three core modules exhibit unique numerical failure patterns: …
